sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The NEXUS Criteria (National Emergency X-Radiography Utilisation Study) are a validated clinical decision aid that can help identify low-risk alert, stable blunt-trauma patients who may not require cervical spine imaging. It is an adjunct to clinical judgement and should not be applied outside the rule's scope.

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content:
- The NEXUS Criteria were developed to help clinicians determine whether cervical spine imaging may be safely avoided in appropriate blunt-trauma patients
- The five low-risk criteria are: no midline cervical tenderness, no focal neurological deficit, normal alertness, no intoxication, and no painful distracting injury
- NEXUS should be used cautiously in older adults and does not replace clinical judgement or local spinal-motion-restriction guidance
- Other validated tools such as the Canadian C-Spine Rule also exist, but whichever tool is used should be applied correctly and within scope
